title: Fix several FreeBSD integration problems
body:
The main goal of this PR is to provide a stub implementation of the containers/stats API endpoint. For now most of the fields are not populated, but this is still useful enough to enable the use of podman+FreeBSD in some container orchestration frameworks.

Along the way I fixed a couple of related FreeBSD-specific bugs.

Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
```release-note
The /libpod/containers/stats endpoint now provides some info about container resource utilization on FreeBSD.
```

comments.body: @dfr I wonder if you could take a look at this? I'm not completely sure about the jailName() change in particular, though it provides the desired results in my testing.
Looks good to me. @markjdb have you tested reading stats for containers inside pods via the API with this change? I think this should work but it would be nice to check that.

you need to add a release note.  in your github message, add:

```
"```release-note
something
```"
```

where something is a description of your changes if users need to be made aware.  `None` is a valid something as well.

> Looks good to me. @markjdb have you tested reading stats for containers inside pods via the API with this change? I think this should work but it would be nice to check that.

I haven't tried that. Do you happen to have a quick recipe for how I'd set that up? I'm still quite new to podman.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

> > Looks good to me. @markjdb have you tested reading stats for containers inside pods via the API with this change? I think this should work but it would be nice to check that.
> 
> I haven't tried that. Do you happen to have a quick recipe for how I'd set that up? I'm still quite new to podman.

You can run a container inside a pod using something like:
```
# podman run -d -t --pod=new:mytestpod ghcr.io/freebsd/freebsd-runtime:14.3
```
If `mytestpod` already exists, remove the `new:` part.

This runs `/bin/sh` inside the container with a tty console (`-t`) and detaches from the console. Use `podman attach` to interact with the shell. If you just want interactive access to the shell immediately, change `-d` to `-i`.
looks like you need to rebase ... @dfr if you give a LGTM to the code, I can then do a quick review and merge.

This fails for me:

```
git (main) markj@xinde> sudo bin/podman run -d -t --pod=new:mytestpod ghcr.io/freebsd/freebsd-runtime:14.3
ERRO[0000] Starting some container dependencies         
ERRO[0000] "rootfs-overlay: creating overlay failed \"/var/run/libpod/infra-container\": read/write overlay mounts not supported on freebsd" 
Error: starting some containers: internal libpod error
```

> looks like you need to rebase ... @dfr if you give a LGTM to the code, I can then do a quick review and merge.

I've rebased, thank you for taking a look.

> ERRO[0000] Starting some container dependencies

> Error: starting some containers: internal libpod error
> ```
> 
>  > looks like you need to rebase ... @dfr if you give a LGTM to the code, I can then do a quick review and merge.
> 
> I've rebased, thank you for taking a look.

The failure is completely my fault - I keep forgetting to follow up on #26188. Currently we work around this using a simple patch in the podman port:
```
--- libpod/container_internal_common.go.orig	2025-06-18 16:07:27 UTC
+++ libpod/container_internal_common.go
@@ -192,7 +192,7 @@ func (c *Container) createInitRootfs() error {
 	}

 	c.config.Rootfs = tmpDir
-	c.config.RootfsOverlay = true
+	//c.config.RootfsOverlay = true
 	return nil
 }

```
LGTM, @mheon quick peek and merge please

> The failure is completely my fault - I keep forgetting to follow up on #26188. Currently we work around this using a simple patch in the podman port:

Thanks! With that change the stats endpoint seems to work as I'd expect: I started several containers in a pod and saw that CPU usage stats get aggregated when querying the pod itself, and I can still query individual containers and get the expected output.

/lgtm

I confess I'd forgotten we had `goto` in Golang until this. Thought we just had break labels.
